title: Source registry
description: The source registry and fetch allowlist for this project's research gnome (platform ADR-0025, docs/source-registry.md). Nothing off this registry is ever fetched. Adapted from kdc's reference schema with one change: cadence and parser are required only for status:active sources, because this project's registry is entirely manual (see ADR-0046) and a fetch cadence for a source that is never fetched is a fiction.

type.description: What the source is, in this domain's terms. maker = the company that builds the thing; review/community = the publications and the people who know it best; retailer = a shop; press = general or trade press cited for corporate and historical facts; investor-relations = corporate primary source for acquisitions.

url.description: The source's root. For status:active this is the exact URL the fetch layer requests; for status:manual it is where a human goes.

parser.description: How the fetch layer itemizes the response. Meaningless for manual sources.

publisher.description: Publisher name as it should appear on signals extracted from this source.

scope.description: What this source covers — for humans reviewing the registry.

status.description: active = fetched on schedule; manual = NEVER fetched (robots/ToS, or no machine-readable surface) — material arrives by hand through research issues; retired = kept for provenance, never fetched.

checked.description: When robots.txt and ToS were last reviewed, by reading them.

notes.description: What robots.txt and the ToS actually say, and the resulting judgment. For manual sources this field must state WHY the source stays manual.

ai_crawler_directives.description: Whether robots.txt names AI/LLM crawlers (GPTBot, CCBot, ClaudeBot, anthropic-ai, Google-Extended) and what it says about them. 'none' is a finding worth recording, not an absence worth omitting.

rate_limit_s.description: Per-host politeness delay; honor any published Crawl-delay.

then.description: A source that is actually fetched must declare how often and how it is parsed.
